steps.title: Shift the input right
steps.content:
A shift $9$ units to the right replaces $x$ with $x-9$ inside the function. This changes the denominator from $x$ to $x-9$ but does not change the numerator.
$$f(x-9)=\frac{a-18}{x-9}+6$$

steps.title: Shift the output down
steps.content: A shift $3$ units down subtracts $3$ from the whole output. The constant term changes from $6$ to $3$ after the vertical shift. $$g(x)=\frac{a-18}{x-9}+6-3$$ $$g(x)=\frac{a-18}{x-9}+3$$ The answer is B.
